title: selinum depauperatum
intro: Selinum depauperatum, also known as poor man's pepper or weak hemlock, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an expectorant to ease respiratory problems. Selinum depauperatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
